title: Robust face detection
abstract: A neural network may implement a face detection process (250) on a mobile device or computer system. An image captured using a camera on the device may be processed using the face detection process (250). The face detection process may provide a bounding box for the face detected in the image. The face detected in the image may have any orientation. The face detected in the image may include either a whole face of the user or only a partial face of the user. The face detection process may provide face detection that is substantially invariant to exposure in the captured image. The face may also be detected over a wide range of distances between the camera and the face of the user.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method, comprising: capturing at least one image using a camera located on a device, the device comprising a computer processor and a memory, wherein the at least one image comprises an image captured while illuminating subjects in the image with an illuminator located on the device; encoding the at least one image to generate feature vectors defining features of subjects in the image in a feature space; assessing the generated feature vectors in the feature space to detect a face in the image, wherein at least some part of the face is present in the image, and wherein the face has any orientation in the image; forming a bounding box around the face detected in the image, wherein, if a portion of the face detected is located outside an image boundary, the bounding box is formed around the at least some part of the face detected to be present in the image; and providing an output, the output comprising the bounding box formed for the detected face in the image. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the computer processor comprises a multi-scale convolutional neural network module. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein assessing the generated feature vectors to detect the face in the image comprises determining the feature vectors that represent the face in the image. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein forming the bounding box comprises forming the bounding box around the feature vectors determined to represent face in the image. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein assessing the generated feature vectors to detect the face in the image comprises classifying the feature vectors to determine the feature vectors that represent the face in the image. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein the output comprises an indication that the face is detected in the image. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein the output comprises an assessed pose of the face in the image and an assessed distance from the camera of the face in the image. 8. A device, comprising: a camera; an illuminator; a memory configured to store program instructions; and a processor configured to execute the program instructions and to cause the device to: capture at least one image using the camera, wherein the at least one image comprises an image captured while illuminating subjects in the image with the illuminator; encode the at least one image to generate feature vectors defining features of subjects in the image in a feature space; assess the generated feature vectors in the feature space to detect a face in the image, wherein at least some part of the face is present in the image, and wherein the face is detected regardless of an orientation of the face with respect to the camera; form a bounding box around the face detected in the image, wherein, if a portion of the face detected is located outside an image boundary, the bounding box is formed around the at least some part of the face detected to be present in the image; and provide an output comprising the bounding box formed for the detected face in the image. 9. The device of claim 8, wherein the camera comprises an infrared sensor. 10. The device of claim 8, wherein the program instructions comprise classifying parameters determined during a training process, and wherein the processor is configured to execute the program instructions and to cause the device to assess the generated feature vectors using the classifying parameters. 11. The device of claim 8, wherein the processor is further configured to execute the program instructions and to cause the device to operate a facial recognition process using the bounding box formed for the detected face in the image. 12. The device of claim 8, wherein the illuminator comprises a flood infrared illuminator. 13. The device of claim 8, wherein the processor is configured to form the bounding box around the face detected in an image captured with any exposure in a range set by exposure limits of the camera on the device. 14. A method, comprising: capturing at least one image using a camera located on a device, the device comprising a computer processor and a memory, wherein the at least one image comprises an image captured while illuminating subjects in the image with an illuminator located on the device; encoding the at least one image to generate feature vectors defining features of subjects in the image in a feature space, wherein an exposure in the at least one image is in an exposure range set by exposure limits of the camera on the device; assessing the generated feature vectors in the feature space to detect a face in the image, wherein at least some part of the face is present in the image; forming a bounding box around the face detected in the image; and providing an output, the output comprising the bounding box formed for the detected face in the image. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the at least one image is captured in challenging lighting conditions. 16. The method of claim 14, wherein the computer processor comprises a multi-scale convolutional neural network module. 17. The method of claim 14, wherein assessing the generated feature vectors to detect the face in the image comprises determining the feature vectors that represent the face in the image. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein forming the bounding box comprises forming the bounding box around the feature vectors determined to represent face in the image. 19. The method of claim 14, wherein assessing the generated feature vectors to detect the face in the image comprises classifying the feature vectors to determine the feature vectors that represent the face in the image. 20. The method of claim 14, wherein the output comprises an indication that the face is detected in the image.
